findings: Strong linear dependencies with correlation near or equal to 1 observed consistently across features e13 to e22.
Dependency profiles are concentrated into narrow structural clusters indicating redundancy among these feature pairs.
No evidence of instability or temporal variation in dependency metrics was found in the localized context.
limitations: Analysis limited to local dependency clusters without cross-segment aggregation.
No direct distribution verification performed due to bounded step budget.

findings: Several feature pairs (e.g., Average Packet Size and Packet Length Mean; Fwd Header Length and Fwd Header Length.1) exhibit near-perfect linear correlation indicating redundancy.
Strong dependencies are attributable to preprocessing or feature engineering rather than inherent protocol structure.
Local consistency verification revealed moderate label conflicts but did not undermine redundancy classification.
Features e13 to e22 exhibit strong linear dependencies with correlation values near or equal to 1, indicating significant redundancy.
Cardinality verification on sampled features confirms low cardinality consistent with near-constant or highly dependent behavior.
Effective dimensionality reduction is supported by the presence of these strong dependencies, reducing the independent feature space within this cluster.
limitations: Cardinality verification was performed on a subset of features (e13, e14, e15) as representative evidence; full feature set cardinality was not exhaustively verified.
